danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based enemy creatures in obstacle course levels
danger_signals.meaning: Hazards to avoid; primary threat in platforming sequences
World-state danger manifested through level design; navigational threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss character appearing between levels with facial animations
danger_signals.meaning: Narrative antagonist; visual punctuation of story progression and escalating supernatural threat

safety_signals.meaning: Safe state: player agency in choosing Ghostbuster variant for level challenges
Safe progression marker; narrative advance between hazardous segments

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Obstacle course level design reflects Ghostbuster team assignments and narrative progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Vigo the Carpathian facial representation in cutscenes
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ghost enemy sprites in platforming levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level themes tied to film narrative (Railway level, Water Front, Wall Street locations suggest urban supernatural invasion)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vigo the Carpathian cutscenes between levels with 'funny faces' animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level sequence follows narrative arc; difficulty/enemy types vary by stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Current Ghostbuster team member name displayed (replaces unused level labels)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character selection determines playable variant for obstacle course segment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unused graphics (skull/face in Level 1, chair in courtroom level) indicate cut content
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unfinished driving levels (Wall Street, Water Front) with glitched graphics; missing Level 4 with Peter Venkman

environmental_storytelling.example: Railway level (Level 3), Water Front, Wall Street — reflect Ghostbusters II film setting and narrative geography of supernatural crisis spreading through NYC

environmental_storytelling.technique: Obstacle course design as character challenge
environmental_storytelling.example: Each Ghostbuster has dedicated on-foot platforming level reflecting their role/abilities in film narrative
Vigo appears between levels as narrative anchor, visually reinforcing antagonist presence and threat escalation
Unfinished driving levels and missing Level 4 suggest more expansive NYC exploration was planned; courtroom reference indicates story beats from film were considered for gameplay

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appears in cutscenes with animated facial expressions ('funny faces'); serves as visual narrative device without direct gameplay interaction
Sprite-based platforming obstacles; hazards to navigate/avoid
Name displayed; character variant controls obstacle course navigation

notes: TCRF source reveals development cuts: unused Level 1 skull/face graphic, unused courtroom chair sprite, incomplete driving levels (Wall Street, Water Front) with glitched rendering, and missing Level 4 (Peter Venkman apartment infiltration). These suggest the final game represents condensed/altered version of larger planned experience. Vigo serves as primary visual/narrative connector across fragmented level structure. No color-coded faction system or symbolic sigils documented; faction identity relies on character roster and cutscene presence rather than environmental visual language. Game structure: character select → platforming level → Vigo cutscene → next stage.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Ghostbusters II NES)
